page: Amazon Web Services c5d.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 8124M core with hyper-threading. The processor operates at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture and is supported by 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. This instance is built on the Nitro hypervisor and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1484.88 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1122.0. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression at 23.82 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 8.89 GB/sec also align with average tiers. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are poor, reflecting the single physical core limitation. The multi-core stress-ng score is 1953.77, and large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 12.85 GB/sec.

The c5d.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-focused tasks requiring fast local scratch space, thanks to its bundled NVMe SSD. However, the 4.0 GB memory capacity and poor multi-threaded scaling present clear tradeoffs for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for single-threaded compute tasks, lightweight web serving, or development workloads that benefit from high-speed local storage but do not require extensive memory or parallel processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1122.0, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are poor due to the single physical core constraint. This instance provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring fast local storage and single-threaded compute, but its low memory capacity and limited multi-core scaling make it less suitable for parallel processing. It is best utilized for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and basic development tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average single-core and poor multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: An entry-level compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded workloads.

50 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
